noster&quot;,&quot;title-short&quot;:&quot;The politics of
Jesus&quot;,&quot;author&quot;:[{&quot;family&quot;:&quot;Yoder&quot;,&quot;given&quot;:&quot;John
Howard&quot;}],&quot;issued&quot;:{&quot;date-parts&quot;:[[&quot;1994&quot;]]}}}],&quot;schema&quot;:&quot;https://github.com/citation-style-language/schema/raw/master/csl-citation.json&quot;}
<span style='mso-element:field-separator'></span><![endif]-->Yoder.<!--[if supportFields]><span
style='mso-element:field-end'></span><![endif]--> Pg. 44<o:p></o:p></p>

</div>

<div id="ftn24">

<p class="MsoFootnoteText" style="text-indent: .5in;"><a href="file:///C:/Users/darre/Google%20Drive/School_Darrell/-TKU/Courses/Life%20of%20Jesus%20-%20BIBL2302ONL2/RESEARCH%20-%20POLITICAL%20JESUS/Research%20Paper_Life%20of%20Jesus_Wolfe,%20Darrell%20Final.docx#_ftnref24" name="_ftn24" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-bidi-theme-font: minor-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[24]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a>
<!--[if supportFields]><span style='mso-element:field-begin'></span><span
style='mso-spacerun:yes'> </span>ADDIN ZOTERO_ITEM CSL_CITATION
{&quot;citationID&quot;:&quot;u4LpXNPj&quot;,&quot;properties&quot;:{&quot;formattedCitation&quot;:&quot;Perrin,
Brown, and Green, {\\i{}DJG}.&quot;,&quot;plainCitation&quot;:&quot;Perrin,
Brown, and Green,
DJG.&quot;,&quot;noteIndex&quot;:23},&quot;citationItems&quot;:[{&quot;id&quot;:472,&quot;uris&quot;:[&quot;http://zotero.org/users/6953150/items/VV9Y4R2F&quot;],&quot;uri&quot;:[&quot;http://zotero.org/users/6953150/items/VV9Y4R2F&quot;],&quot;itemData&quot;:{&quot;id&quot;:472,&quot;type&quot;:&quot;book&quot;,&quot;abstract&quot;:&quot;1993
ECPA Gold Medallion (Reference Text) 1993 Christianity Today Critics'Choice
Award The 2014 ECPA Christian Book Award Winner (Bible Reference) 2014
Readers'Choice Award Winner Preaching's Preacher's Guide to the Best Bible Reference
for 2014 (Gospels and Jesus) The second edition of the Dictionary of Jesus and
the Gospels is a thoroughly reconstructed and revised version of the critically
acclaimed 1992 first edition. Since that groundbreaking volume was published, a
wave of Jesus and Gospel scholarship has crested and broken on the shores of a
new century. Jesus has been proposed as sage, shaman, revolutionary, marginal
Jew, Mediterranean peasant or a prophet of Israel's restoration. The
non-canonical Gospels have been touted, examined and reassessed. There are
revised understandings of historiography, orality, form criticism, empire and
more. The second edition of the DJG amply weighs and assess the gains and
shortcomings of this new scholarship. Here is a self-contained reference
library of information and perspective essential to exploring Jesus and the
Gospels. This volume bridges the gap between scholars and those pastors,
teachers, students and interested readers who want thorough treatments of key
topics in an accessible and summary format. Articles cover each Gospel, major
themes in the Gospels, key episodes in the life of Jesus, significant
background topics, as well as issues and methods of interpretation. Among other
benefits, it allows multiple opportunities for each of the Gospels to be
weighed and heard in its own voice. Bibliographies are full and up to date,
putting readers in touch with the best work in the field. All of this allows
the articles to serve as launching pads for further research. When the first
edition of the Dictionary of Jesus and the Gospels was published, it was
immediately recognized as an innovative reference work. By taking a particular
corpus of biblical books and exploring it with in-depth articles written by
specialists in the field, it refashioned a staple reference genre. This
dictionary model has now been applied to each segment of the biblical canon in
successive volumes. Those who have enjoyed and benefitted from the wealth in
the first edition will find the second edition an equally indispensable companion
to study and research. Over ninety percent of the articles have been completely
rewritten, and the rest thoroughly revised and updated. Here is the doorway
into a reliable and comprehensive summary and appraisal of the last twenty
years of Jesus scholarship. A new generation of scholars has opened the way to
make this a Dictionary of Jesus and the Gospels for the twenty-first
century.&quot;,&quot;collection-number&quot;:&quot;K is for Kingdom; Judaism,
Common&quot;,&quot;collection-title&quot;:&quot;IVP Bible Dictionary
Series&quot;,&quot;event-place&quot;:&quot;Downers Grove,
Ill&quot;,&quot;ISBN&quot;:&quot;978-0-8308-2456-4&quot;,&quot;language&quot;:&quot;English&quot;,&quot;publisher&quot;:&quot;IVP
Academic&quot;,&quot;publisher-place&quot;:&quot;Downers Grove,
Ill&quot;,&quot;source&quot;:&quot;EBSCOhost&quot;,&quot;title&quot;:&quot;Dictionary
of Jesus and the Gospels
(DJG)&quot;,&quot;title-short&quot;:&quot;DJG&quot;,&quot;URL&quot;:&quot;http://search.ebscohost.com/login.aspx?direct=true&amp;db=nlebk&amp;AN=633424&amp;site=ehost-live&quot;,&quot;author&quot;:[{&quot;family&quot;:&quot;Perrin&quot;,&quot;given&quot;:&quot;Nicholas&quot;},{&quot;family&quot;:&quot;Brown&quot;,&quot;given&quot;:&quot;Jeannine
K.&quot;},{&quot;family&quot;:&quot;Green&quot;,&quot;given&quot;:&quot;Joel
B.&quot;}],&quot;accessed&quot;:{&quot;date-parts&quot;:[[&quot;2020&quot;,11,23]]},&quot;issued&quot;:{&quot;date-parts&quot;:[[&quot;2013&quot;]]}}}],&quot;schema&quot;:&quot;https://github.com/citation-style-language/schema/raw/master/csl-citation.json&quot;}
<span style='mso-element:field-separator'></span><![endif]-->Perrin,
Brown, and Green, <i>DJG</i>.<!--[if supportFields]><span
style='mso-element:field-end'></span><![endif]--><o:p></o:p></p>

</div>

<div id="ftn25">

<p class="MsoFootnoteText" style="text-indent: .5in;"><a href="file:///C:/Users/darre/Google%20Drive/School_Darrell/-TKU/Courses/Life%20of%20Jesus%20-%20BIBL2302ONL2/RESEARCH%20-%20POLITICAL%20JESUS/Research%20Paper_Life%20of%20Jesus_Wolfe,%20Darrell%20Final.docx#_ftnref25" name="_ftn25" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-bidi-theme-font: minor-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[25]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a>
<!--[if supportFields]><span style='mso-element:field-begin'></span><span
style='mso-spacerun:yes'> </span>ADDIN ZOTERO_ITEM CSL_CITATION
{&quot;citationID&quot;:&quot;6CXw0WfZ&quot;,&quot;properties&quot;:{&quot;formattedCitation&quot;:&quot;Immanuel
Maurice Wallerstein, \\uc0\\u8220{}Render unto Caesar?: The Dilemmas of a
Multicultural World,\\uc0\\u8221{} {\\i{}Sociology of Religion} 66, no. 2
(2005): 121\\uc0\\u8211{}33.&quot;,&quot;plainCitation&quot;:&quot;Immanuel
Maurice Wallerstein, “Render unto Caesar?: The Dilemmas of a Multicultural
World,” Sociology of Religion 66, no. 2 (2005):
121–33.&quot;,&quot;noteIndex&quot;:17},&quot;citationItems&quot;:[{&quot;id&quot;:446,&quot;uris&quot;:[&quot;http://zotero.org/users/6953150/items/J3CIXATI&quot;],&quot;uri&quot;:[&quot;http://zotero.org/users/6953150/items/J3CIXATI&quot;],&quot;itemData&quot;:{&quot;id&quot;:446,&quot;type&quot;:&quot;article-journal&quot;,&quot;container-title&quot;:&quot;Sociology
of
Religion&quot;,&quot;ISSN&quot;:&quot;1069-4404&quot;,&quot;issue&quot;:&quot;2&quot;,&quot;journalAbbreviation&quot;:&quot;Sociology
of Religion&quot;,&quot;page&quot;:&quot;121-133&quot;,&quot;source&quot;:&quot;EBSCOhost&quot;,&quot;title&quot;:&quot;Render
unto Caesar?: the dilemmas of a multicultural world&quot;,&quot;title-short&quot;:&quot;Render
unto
Caesar?&quot;,&quot;volume&quot;:&quot;66&quot;,&quot;author&quot;:[{&quot;family&quot;:&quot;Wallerstein&quot;,&quot;given&quot;:&quot;Immanuel
Maurice&quot;}],&quot;issued&quot;:{&quot;date-parts&quot;:[[&quot;2005&quot;]]}}}],&quot;schema&quot;:&quot;https://github.com/citation-style-language/schema/raw/master/csl-citation.json&quot;}
<span style='mso-element:field-separator'></span><![endif]-->Immanuel
Maurice Wallerstein, “Render unto Caesar?: The Dilemmas of a Multicultural
World,” <i>Sociology of Religion</i> 66, no. 2 (2005): 121–33.<!--[if supportFields]><span
style='mso-element:field-end'></span><![endif]--><o:p></o:p></p>

</div>

<div id="ftn26">

<p class="MsoFootnoteText" style="text-indent: .5in;"><a href="file:///C:/Users/darre/Google%20Drive/School_Darrell/-TKU/Courses/Life%20of%20Jesus%20-%20BIBL2302ONL2/RESEARCH%20-%20POLITICAL%20JESUS/Research%20Paper_Life%20of%20Jesus_Wolfe,%20Darrell%20Final.docx#_ftnref26" name="_ftn26" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-bidi-theme-font: minor-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[26]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a>
<!--[if supportFields]><span style='mso-element:field-begin'></span><span
style='mso-spacerun:yes'> </span>ADDIN ZOTERO_ITEM CSL_CITATION
{&quot;citationID&quot;:&quot;IMaUf0Gy&quot;,&quot;properties&quot;:{&quot;formattedCitation&quot;:&quot;Skye
Jethani, Holy Post Podcast, Unknown, https://www.holypost.com/.&quot;,&quot;plainCitation&quot;:&quot;Skye
Jethani, Holy Post Podcast, Unknown,
https://www.holypost.com/.&quot;,&quot;noteIndex&quot;:25},&quot;citationItems&quot;:[{&quot;id&quot;:538,&quot;uris&quot;:[&quot;http://zotero.org/users/6953150/items/KQ9ZF46C&quot;],&quot;uri&quot;:[&quot;http://zotero.org/users/6953150/items/KQ9ZF46C&quot;],&quot;itemData&quot;:{&quot;id&quot;:538,&quot;type&quot;:&quot;interview&quot;,&quot;title&quot;:&quot;Holy
Post
Podcast&quot;,&quot;URL&quot;:&quot;https://www.holypost.com/&quot;,&quot;author&quot;:[{&quot;family&quot;:&quot;Jethani&quot;,&quot;given&quot;:&quot;Skye&quot;}],&quot;issued&quot;:{&quot;literal&quot;:&quot;Unknown&quot;}}}],&quot;schema&quot;:&quot;https://github.com/citation-style-language/schema/raw/master/csl-citation.json&quot;}
<span style='mso-element:field-separator'></span><![endif]-->Skye Jethani, Holy Post Podcast,
Unknown, https://www.holypost.com/.<!--[if supportFields]><span
style='mso-element:field-end'></span><![endif]--> I recently heard Jethani say
something like this; however, I cannot find the quote or podcast episode. I
want to ensure I give him credit for inspiring the idea.<o:p></o:p></p>

</div>

<div id="ftn27">

<p class="MsoFootnoteText" style="text-indent: .5in;"><a href="file:///C:/Users/darre/Google%20Drive/School_Darrell/-TKU/Courses/Life%20of%20Jesus%20-%20BIBL2302ONL2/RESEARCH%20-%20POLITICAL%20JESUS/Research%20Paper_Life%20of%20Jesus_Wolfe,%20Darrell%20Final.docx#_ftnref27" name="_ftn27" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-bidi-theme-font: minor-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[27]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a>
<!--[if supportFields]><span style='mso-element:field-begin'></span><span
style='mso-spacerun:yes'> </span>ADDIN ZOTERO_ITEM CSL_CITATION
{&quot;citationID&quot;:&quot;aEArrCDb&quot;,&quot;properties&quot;:{&quot;formattedCitation&quot;:&quot;Richard
E Oster, \\uc0\\u8220{}\\uc0\\u8216{}Show Me a Denarius\\uc0\\u8217{}: Symbolism
of Roman Coinage and Christian Beliefs,\\uc0\\u8221{} {\\i{}Restoration
Quarterly} 28, no. 2 (1985):
107\\uc0\\u8211{}15.&quot;,&quot;plainCitation&quot;:&quot;Richard E Oster,
“‘Show Me a Denarius’: Symbolism of Roman Coinage and Christian Beliefs,”
Restoration Quarterly 28, no. 2 (1985): 107–15.&quot;,&quot;noteIndex&quot;:18},&quot;citationItems&quot;:[{&quot;id&quot;:534,&quot;uris&quot;:[&quot;http://zotero.org/users/6953150/items/XPGPYUAC&quot;],&quot;uri&quot;:[&quot;http://zotero.org/users/6953150/items/XPGPYUAC&quot;],&quot;itemData&quot;:{&quot;id&quot;:534,&quot;type&quot;:&quot;article-journal&quot;,&quot;call-number&quot;:&quot;ATLA0000968471&quot;,&quot;container-title&quot;:&quot;Restoration
Quarterly&quot;,&quot;ISSN&quot;:&quot;0486-5642&quot;,&quot;issue&quot;:&quot;2&quot;,&quot;journalAbbreviation&quot;:&quot;Restoration

me a denarius': symbolism of Roman coinage and Christian

denarius'&quot;,&quot;volume&quot;:&quot;28&quot;,&quot;author&quot;:[{&quot;family&quot;:&quot;Oster&quot;,&quot;given&quot;:&quot;Richard
E&quot;}],&quot;issued&quot;:{&quot;date-parts&quot;:[[&quot;1985&quot;]]}}}],&quot;schema&quot;:&quot;https://github.com/citation-style-language/schema/raw/master/csl-citation.json&quot;}

E Oster, “‘Show Me a Denarius’: Symbolism of Roman Coinage and Christian
Beliefs,” <i>Restoration Quarterly</i> 28, no. 2 (1985): 107–15.<!--[if supportFields]><span
style='mso-element:field-end'></span><![endif]--><o:p></o:p></p>

</div>

<div id="ftn28">

<p class="MsoFootnoteText" style="text-indent: .5in;"><a href="file:///C:/Users/darre/Google%20Drive/School_Darrell/-TKU/Courses/Life%20of%20Jesus%20-%20BIBL2302ONL2/RESEARCH%20-%20POLITICAL%20JESUS/Research%20Paper_Life%20of%20Jesus_Wolfe,%20Darrell%20Final.docx#_ftnref28" name="_ftn28" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-bidi-theme-font: minor-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[28]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a>
<!--[if supportFields]><span style='mso-element:field-begin'></span><span
style='mso-spacerun:yes'> </span>ADDIN ZOTERO_ITEM CSL_CITATION
{&quot;citationID&quot;:&quot;eOZNEhaV&quot;,&quot;properties&quot;:{&quot;formattedCitation&quot;:&quot;Josephus
Flavius, \\uc0\\u8220{}Roman Currency,\\uc0\\u8221{} in {\\i{}Josephus Flavius:
Complete Works and Historical Background (Annotated and Illustrated) (Annotated
Classics) Kindle Edition}, trans. William Whiston (Annotated Classics, 2013),
800,
https://www.amazon.com/Josephus-Flavius-Historical-Background-Illustrated-ebook/dp/B00B4I367Q.&quot;,&quot;plainCitation&quot;:&quot;Josephus
Flavius, “Roman Currency,” in Josephus Flavius: Complete Works and Historical
Background (Annotated and Illustrated) (Annotated Classics) Kindle Edition,
trans. William Whiston (Annotated Classics, 2013), 800,
https://www.amazon.com/Josephus-Flavius-Historical-Background-Illustrated-ebook/dp/B00B4I367Q.&quot;,&quot;noteIndex&quot;:16},&quot;citationItems&quot;:[{&quot;id&quot;:533,&quot;uris&quot;:[&quot;http://zotero.org/users/6953150/items/344YWQVZ&quot;],&quot;uri&quot;:[&quot;http://zotero.org/users/6953150/items/344YWQVZ&quot;],&quot;itemData&quot;:{&quot;id&quot;:533,&quot;type&quot;:&quot;chapter&quot;,&quot;abstract&quot;:&quot;*
Illustrated with historical maps, timelines, graphical family trees of Jewish
leaders, images of coinage, as well as models of contemporary Jerusalem,
Solomon's Temple, and Herod's Temple\n* Annotated with concise introduction,
that includes modern view of history and analysis of Josephus works\n* Includes
biographies of Josephus, Hasmonaean Dynasty, Herodian Dynasty, Herod,
Vespasian, Titus and Domitian\n* Includes explanation of Hebrew, Greek, and
Roman coinage, units of measure, as well as Hebrew, Julian, and
Syrian/Macedonian calendars.\n*Includes the original text of Josephus'
references to Jesus in Book XVIII and Book XX of Antiquities of the Jews, as
well as description of Essenes.\n* Each book has its own active Table of
Contents.\n* The original footnotes are hyperlinked for easy reference.\n* All
Annotated Classics books are beautifully designed for easy reading and
navigation on e-Readers and mobile
devices.&quot;,&quot;container-title&quot;:&quot;Josephus Flavius: Complete
Works and Historical Background (Annotated and Illustrated) (Annotated
Classics) Kindle Edition&quot;,&quot;ISBN&quot;:&quot;ASIN :
B00B4I367Q&quot;,&quot;page&quot;:&quot;800&quot;,&quot;publisher&quot;:&quot;Annotated
Classics&quot;,&quot;title&quot;:&quot;Roman
Currency&quot;,&quot;URL&quot;:&quot;https://www.amazon.com/Josephus-Flavius-Historical-Background-Illustrated-ebook/dp/B00B4I367Q&quot;,&quot;author&quot;:[{&quot;family&quot;:&quot;Flavius&quot;,&quot;given&quot;:&quot;Josephus&quot;}],&quot;translator&quot;:[{&quot;family&quot;:&quot;Whiston&quot;,&quot;given&quot;:&quot;William&quot;}],&quot;issued&quot;:{&quot;date-parts&quot;:[[&quot;2013&quot;,1,21]]}}}],&quot;schema&quot;:&quot;https://github.com/citation-style-language/schema/raw/master/csl-citation.json&quot;}
<span style='mso-element:field-separator'></span><![endif]-->Josephus
Flavius, “Roman Currency,” in <i>Josephus Flavius: Complete Works and
Historical Background (Annotated and Illustrated) (Annotated Classics) Kindle
Edition</i>, trans. William Whiston (Annotated Classics, 2013), 800,
https://www.amazon.com/Josephus-Flavius-Historical-Background-Illustrated-ebook/dp/B00B4I367Q.<!--[if supportFields]><span
style='mso-element:field-end'></span><![endif]--><o:p></o:p></p>

</div>

<div id="ftn29">

<p class="MsoFootnoteText" style="text-indent: .5in;"><a href="file:///C:/Users/darre/Google%20Drive/School_Darrell/-TKU/Courses/Life%20of%20Jesus%20-%20BIBL2302ONL2/RESEARCH%20-%20POLITICAL%20JESUS/Research%20Paper_Life%20of%20Jesus_Wolfe,%20Darrell%20Final.docx#_ftnref29" name="_ftn29" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-bidi-theme-font: minor-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[29]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a>
<!--[if supportFields]><span style='mso-element:field-begin'></span><span
style='mso-spacerun:yes'> </span>ADDIN ZOTERO_ITEM CSL_CITATION
{&quot;citationID&quot;:&quot;jY8M0How&quot;,&quot;properties&quot;:{&quot;formattedCitation&quot;:&quot;Ann
Spangler and Lois Tverberg, {\\i{}Sitting at the Feet of Rabbi Jesus: How the
Jewishness of Jesus Can Transform Your Faith}, Updated edition (Grand Rapids,
Michigan: Zondervan, 2018).&quot;,&quot;plainCitation&quot;:&quot;Ann Spangler
and Lois Tverberg, Sitting at the Feet of Rabbi Jesus: How the Jewishness of
Jesus Can Transform Your Faith, Updated edition (Grand Rapids, Michigan:
Zondervan, 2018).&quot;,&quot;noteIndex&quot;:28},&quot;citationItems&quot;:[{&quot;id&quot;:186,&quot;uris&quot;:[&quot;http://zotero.org/users/6953150/items/VFB8VX5A&quot;],&quot;uri&quot;:[&quot;http://zotero.org/users/6953150/items/VFB8VX5A&quot;],&quot;itemData&quot;:{&quot;id&quot;:186,&quot;type&quot;:&quot;book&quot;,&quot;abstract&quot;:&quot;Sitting
at the Feet of Rabbi Jesus takes readers on a fascinating journey, helping them
discover how learning about the Jewish world of Jesus can enrich their own
faith. This newly expanded softcover edition includes a discussion guide and a
Jewish Seder liturgy&quot;,&quot;call-number&quot;:&quot;BT590.J8 S63
2018&quot;,&quot;edition&quot;:&quot;Updated edition&quot;,&quot;event-place&quot;:&quot;Grand
Rapids,
Michigan&quot;,&quot;ISBN&quot;:&quot;978-0-310-33069-1&quot;,&quot;number-of-pages&quot;:&quot;301&quot;,&quot;publisher&quot;:&quot;Zondervan&quot;,&quot;publisher-place&quot;:&quot;Grand
Rapids, Michigan&quot;,&quot;source&quot;:&quot;Library of Congress
ISBN&quot;,&quot;title&quot;:&quot;Sitting at the Feet of Rabbi Jesus: How the
Jewishness of Jesus Can Transform Your
Faith&quot;,&quot;title-short&quot;:&quot;Sitting at the feet of Rabbi
Jesus&quot;,&quot;author&quot;:[{&quot;family&quot;:&quot;Spangler&quot;,&quot;given&quot;:&quot;Ann&quot;},{&quot;family&quot;:&quot;Tverberg&quot;,&quot;given&quot;:&quot;Lois&quot;}],&quot;issued&quot;:{&quot;date-parts&quot;:[[&quot;2018&quot;]]}}}],&quot;schema&quot;:&quot;https://github.com/citation-style-language/schema/raw/master/csl-citation.json&quot;}
<span style='mso-element:field-separator'></span><![endif]-->Ann
Spangler and Lois Tverberg, <i>Sitting at the Feet of Rabbi Jesus: How the
Jewishness of Jesus Can Transform Your Faith</i>, Updated edition (Grand
Rapids, Michigan: Zondervan, 2018).<!--[if supportFields]><span
style='mso-element:field-end'></span><![endif]--><o:p></o:p></p>

</div>

<div id="ftn30">

<p class="MsoFootnoteText" style="text-indent: .5in;"><a href="file:///C:/Users/darre/Google%20Drive/School_Darrell/-TKU/Courses/Life%20of%20Jesus%20-%20BIBL2302ONL2/RESEARCH%20-%20POLITICAL%20JESUS/Research%20Paper_Life%20of%20Jesus_Wolfe,%20Darrell%20Final.docx#_ftnref30" name="_ftn30" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-bidi-theme-font: minor-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[30]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a>
<!--[if supportFields]><span style='mso-element:field-begin'></span><span
style='mso-spacerun:yes'> </span>ADDIN ZOTERO_ITEM CSL_CITATION {&quot;citationID&quot;:&quot;HVnEDuSj&quot;,&quot;properties&quot;:{&quot;formattedCitation&quot;:&quot;Thomas

passage showcases Jesus' rhetorical prowess like few other passages do. But to
see and understand how it works, we have to turn to the Old
Testament.&quot;,&quot;container-title&quot;:&quot;The Chi

</div>

<div id="ftn31">
